title: aeschynanthus roseoflorus
intro: Aeschynanthus roseoflorus, also known as the goldfish plant, is a flowering plant species in the family Gesneriaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The goldfish plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, offering benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and in some cases, the leaves are used fresh in salads or cooked in soups, but more research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy in herbal preparations.
